dbbot v0.9.0-beta.1 refreshes the MHA-Go deployment path for current mha-go and validates it against a MySQL 9.7.0 GTID primary-replica lab.

This beta changes `mha_go.yml` into an incremental manager deployment entry point for an existing `master_slave.yml` topology. It no longer reinstalls MySQL, rebuilds replication, or requires the legacy SSH passwordless role before deploying mha-go.

The bundled MHA-Go role now renders the current `mysql-replication-single-primary` topology kind, includes replication credentials for replica repointing, defaults semi-sync checks to `disabled` for dbbot's async `master_slave.yml` topology, and allows the manager to run on either the primary or a replica. The role also validates both `gtid_mode` and `enforce_gtid_consistency`, restarts `mha-manager` when its binary, config, or unit changes, and ships an updated static `mha` binary.

Documentation for dbbot and dbbot.ai has been aligned with the new deployment model, MySQL 8.4.x and 9.7.0 support scope, manager defaults, and the tested three-node lab flow.
